workflow_details.description: Add information that will help to differentiate this workflow from another.
workflow_details.title: Set workflow details

data_source.description: Select one of your configured data sources.

time_range.description: Choose the period of time to analyze.
time_range.title: Define analysis time range

trajectory_segment_filter.description: Filter track data by setting limits on track segment length, duration, and speed. Segments outside these bounds are removed, reducing noise and to focus on meaningful movement patterns.

exaggeration.title: Exaggeration
exaggeration.description: Vertical exaggeration factor. 1.0 = true scale, 2.0 = 2x heights.

head_layer.title: Head Layer
head_layer.description: 3D glTF/GLB head model settings. Enable with the 'enabled' checkbox.

ScenegraphLayerDefinition.description:
An animated 3D head built from a glTF/GLB model (deck.gl ScenegraphLayer).

Create one with create_scenegraph_layer() and pass it to
draw_animated_map(head_layer=...). Its position and heading are driven per-frame
from the TripsLayer, so it follows each subject's current location. `glb` accepts
an http(s) URL, a `data:` URI, or a local file path (read and embedded as a data
URI). None -> bundled default (the elephant model). If the ScenegraphLayer
constructor or the glTF loader can't be resolved at runtime, the head silently
falls back to the flat ScatterplotLayer dot.
See https://deck.gl/docs/api-reference/mesh-layers/scenegraph-layer for more info.

enabled.description: Enable the 3D head model. When off, subjects render as flat dots.
enabled.title: Enabled

glb.description: GLB source: an http(s) URL, a data: URI, or a local file path. None -> bundled default model (elephant).
glb.title: Glb

size_scale.description: ScenegraphLayer sizeScale. Tune to your scene.
size_scale.title: Size Scale

size_min_pixels.description: Clamp the on-screen model to at least this many pixels so it stays visible when zoomed out.
size_min_pixels.title: Size Min Pixels

size_max_pixels.description: Optional upper clamp on the model's on-screen size in pixels.
size_max_pixels.title: Size Max Pixels

face_heading.description: Rotate the model to face its direction of travel.
face_heading.title: Face Heading

yaw_offset.description: Degrees added to the computed heading so the model's nose aligns with travel. Model-dependent; tweak if your model faces sideways.
yaw_offset.title: Yaw Offset

model_pitch.description: Tilt of the MODEL itself (deg), independent of the camera. Use to correct a model authored nose-up/down; NOT the view pitch.
model_pitch.title: Model Pitch

model_roll.description: Bank of the MODEL itself (deg), independent of the camera.
model_roll.title: Model Roll

smooth_samples.description: Heading/slope smoothing window in track fixes (+/- N). Higher = smoother orientation but more lag; 0 = raw single segment.
smooth_samples.title: Smooth Samples

terrain_pitch.description: Tilt the model to the terrain slope (from each fix's z) so it noses up on climbs / down on descents. OFF by default -> stays upright; enable only for steady, climbing tracks (it can tip near-stationary subjects).
terrain_pitch.title: Terrain Pitch

terrain_pitch_scale.description: Sign/strength of terrain pitch. Set -1.0 to flip if the model tilts the wrong way; <1 to soften. (Tilt is also capped at +/-20deg.)
terrain_pitch_scale.title: Terrain Pitch Scale

min_move_m.description: If the subject moves less than this (m) across the smoothing window, hold the last heading and keep the model level -- stops it spinning or tipping while milling in place.
min_move_m.title: Min Move M

tint.description: Optional RGB tint over the model as [R, G, B]. None -> the model's own materials.
tint.title: Tint

use_track_color.description: Colour the model with each subject's track colour. False -> use `tint` (or the model's own materials). Note: the colour multiplies the model's material, so it reads truest with a light/neutral glb and flat lighting (pbr_lighting=False).
use_track_color.title: Use Track Color

ScenegraphLayerDefinition.title: ScenegraphLayerDefinition

auto.description: Match the animation's own playback length. Uncheck to set a fixed duration.
auto.title: Auto

seconds.description: Video duration in seconds. Used when auto is off, or as a fallback when auto cannot determine the length.
seconds.title: Seconds
